DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO PLAINTIFF DAVID CARDONA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. THE COURT AGREES WITH THE DEFENDANT CERTAIN DOCUMENT REQUEST CATEGORIES WHICH SEEK "ANY AND ALL" DOCUMENTS IN A SONG-BEVERLY CONSUMER WARRANTY ACT ACTION IS OVERBROAD AND POTENTIALLY COVERS MILLIONS OF DOCUMENTS. THE COURT INTENDS TO MODIFY, WHEN NEEDED, EACH SPECIFIC DOCUMENT REQUEST CATEGORY TO FOCUS ON ALLEGATIONS OF THE SUBJECT VEHICLE'S ALLEGED TRANSMISSION PROBLEM. THE COURT FINDS DONLEN V. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, 217 CAL. APP. 4TH 138 (2013) AND DOPPES V. BENTLEY MOTORS, INC., 174 CAL. APP 4TH 967 (2009) SUPPORT THE BROAD SCOPE OF DISCOVERABLE DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE REQUESTED. THE COURT AGREES WITH DEFENDANT A PROTECTIVE ORDER/CONFIDENTIALITY ORDER IS APPROPRIATE FOR MANY OF THE DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED. PROPOSED ORDER SHALL SUBMITTED ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 1, 2015. DEFENDANT SHALL BE PERMITTED TO PROVIDE A SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION ON PROTECTIVE ORDER ISSUES. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, REPORTER: KATHRYN LLOYD, CSR# 5955, TEL NO. 925-548-9114 (302/JPT)
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-20-2015 01:30 PM FOR DEPT 302

ATTY. NEIL GIELEGHEM, TEL. NO. 1-310-284-3252 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant's Motion For A Protective Order In Connection With Production Of Documents is granted. Parties to meet and confer as to form of order. Judge Pro tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Kathryn Lloyd, CSR # 5955 =302/jpt

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Docs Set One
Off Calendar Aug-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NEIL GIELEGHEM, ESQ. of the Law Offices of Neil Gieleghem, Tel No. 310-284-3252 and atty. BENJEMAN BECK, ESQ. of Strategic Legal Practices APC, Tel No. 310-277-1040 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. PAUL EFSTRATIS, ESQ. and RYAN J. GREENSPAN, ESQ. of LeClairRyan LLP, Tel No. 415-913-4957 appearing for the Defendant (Mr. Greenspan appearing telephonically).

RULING - Matter argued by counsel. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF DAVID CARDONA'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, the motion to compel is granted. The Court agrees with the defendant certain document request categories which seek "any and all" documents in a Song-Beverly Consumer Warranty Act action is overbroad and potentially covers millions of documents. The Court intends to modify, when needed, each specific document request category to focus on allegations of the subject vehicle's alleged transmission problem. The Court finds Donlen v. Ford Motor Company, 217 Cal. App. 4th 138 (2013) and Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc., 174 Cal. App 4th 967 (2009) support the broad scope of discoverable documentary evidence requested. The Court agrees with defendant a Protective Order/Confidentiality Order is appropriate for many of the documents to be produced. Proposed order shall submitted on or before September 1, 2015. Defendant shall be permitted to provide a supplemental declaration on protective order issues. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: Sean Kane, Reporter: Kathryn Lloyd, CSR# 5955, Tel No. 925-548-9114 (302/JPT)
